topic_definition: Only use title/abstract evidence. Keep source-faithful boundaries: preference learning in audio, possible RL loop for an audio model, audio-domain applicability, and exclusion of preference-only-for-evaluation work.

questions.question: Does the title/abstract indicate ranking, A/B comparison, or other comparative preference signals for audio clips?
If numeric ratings are mentioned, is there observable title/abstract evidence that they are converted into rankings or comparative preference signals?
If explicit comparisons are absent, does the title/abstract indicate an RL training loop for an audio model?
Is the paper applied within the audio domain, including multimodal work that clearly includes audio?
Does the title/abstract directly indicate that preferences are used only for evaluation, with no learning component?
Does the title/abstract clearly indicate that the paper is a survey or review article?
